summary: Static code review of ReservationActions.tsx to verify the cancel-reservation dialog bug fix. All 4 requested checkpoints PASS: (1) the cancel flow no longer uses AlertDialog/AlertDialogTrigger/AlertDialogAction, (2) uses a native <textarea> with autoFocus, (3) buttons invoke the async cancel handlers then closeCancelDialog(), (4) three <CancelDialogModal /> render points exist (pending-no-payment showRefund=false, pending-with-payment showRefund=true, confirmed showRefund=true). One HIGH-priority concern found: CancelDialogModal is defined as a nested component inside ReservationActions, which will remount on every parent re-render and can cause the textarea to lose focus/cursor position on each keystroke.

ui_bugs.issue: CancelDialogModal is declared inside the ReservationActions function body (line 251). Every keystroke updates cancellationReason state -> parent re-renders -> a NEW component function reference is created -> React unmounts and remounts the modal. This can cause the textarea to lose focus mid-typing and defeats the purpose of the fix. Move CancelDialogModal outside the parent (or inline the JSX directly) and pass props explicitly.

action_items: Move CancelDialogModal out of the ReservationActions function body OR inline its JSX in each of the 3 render points to prevent remount-on-keystroke and textarea focus loss.

Remove unused imports now that AlertDialog is no longer used: AlertDialog, AlertDialogAction, AlertDialogCancel, AlertDialogContent, AlertDialogDescription, AlertDialogFooter, AlertDialogHeader, AlertDialogTitle, AlertDialogTrigger (line 3), Textarea (line 8), and FileText (line 7) if unused.
critical_code_review_comments: Nested component definition (CancelDialogModal inside ReservationActions) is a React anti-pattern; guarantees remount on every parent state change. Given the bug being fixed is textarea-typing-related, this is directly relevant and should be refactored.
handleCancelWithRefund / handleCancelWithoutRefund duplicate ~40 lines of Supabase update logic. Extract a shared cancelReservation({ refund: boolean }) helper.
The three JSX branches (lines 318, 341, 355) each render <CancelDialogModal /> - consider rendering it once at the top of the component to avoid three copies of the same modal tree.
Unused shadcn imports (AlertDialog family, Textarea) should be cleaned up to reduce bundle size and reflect the new implementation.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on the 4 requested checkpoints; 1 additional HIGH concern flagged

context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fix replaces shadcn AlertDialog with a custom controlled modal (state: cancelDialogOpen, cancelShowRefund). Verify by opening the app, opening a pending reservation's Cancel button, and typing in the textarea - if characters appear one-at-a-time and focus is lost after each keystroke, the nested-component remount bug is the cause. Also confirm the Sin Devolución / Con Devolución buttons execute the async Supabase update (check console logs '[Cancelar con Devolución] ✅' / '[Cancelar sin Devolución] ✅').
rca of the issue: Original bug: shadcn AlertDialogAction closes the dialog synchronously on click (its onClick default behavior), aborting the async handleCancel* functions before Supabase update completes. Fix: replace with a plain controlled modal using native <button type='button'> so the click no longer auto-closes; the handlers now `await` the update and then explicitly call closeCancelDialog(). Fix is correct in principle. Residual risk: nested-component remount pattern may reintroduce a typing/focus bug in the textarea - see UI bug above.
